ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON JAN-12-2012 IN DEPT. 503, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PER CCP 36(A) CONDITIONED ON PLAINTIFF'S AGREEMENT THAT SUMMARY MOTIONS MAY BE HEARD ON 15 DAYS NOTICE WITHOUT AN ADDITIONAL TWO DAYS FOR E-SERVICE. TRIAL IS APRIL 30, 2012 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 503. LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS APRIL 19, WITH OPPOSITIONS DUE FIVE COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING AND REPLIES DUE TWO COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. PLAINTIFF TO MAKE A SETTLEMENT DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN APRIL 2, 2012. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 1:30PM ON APRIL 19, 2012. FACT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS APRIL 13, 2012. EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS APRIL 27, 2012. NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 10, 2012, PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE A STATEMENT STATING, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT, THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY FACTS SUPPORTING ANY OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 10, 2012, DEFENDANTS MUST PROVIDE A STATEMENT STATING THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY FACTS SUPPORTING ANY OF DEFENDANT'S AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. ANY PERSON NOT IDENTIFIED ON THE STATEMENTS WILL BE BARRED FROM TESTIFYING AT TRIAL OR PRESENTING A DECLARATION ON A SUMMARY MOTION ABSENT GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING ANY OTHER SCHEDULING OR OTHER ISSUE WHICH ANY OF THEM WANT TO HAVE INCLUDED IN THE PREFERENCE ORDER. FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON MARCH 21, 2012 AT 1:30PM. NO NEED FOR ANY PARTY TO FILE ANY CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE STATEMENT. PARTIES MAY APPEAR AT THE CONFERENCE BY TELEPHONE. ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM BY BERRY AND BERRY AND SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (503)
ASBESTOS MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR APR-19-2012 AT 1:30 PM IN DEPT. 603. (503)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE, 503, HEARING HELD. THE COURT SETS A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE ON APRIL 26, 2012 AT 1:30 PM IN DEPARTMENT 503. CLIENTS MUST BE PRESENT OR ON TELEPHONE STANDBY. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE STATEMENTS ARE DUE BY APRIL 23, 2012. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lisa L. Oberg of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation l
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Hubert Welch

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect), denied as to issue 4 (punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). Defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs have dismissed their claim for false representation. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)
